text:
Thank you, Brother Carlson. Peter said on the day of Pentecost, "This is that." If this isn't that, I'd just like to stay with this until that comes. This is wonderful. Who would've ever thought that Christian Businessmen would've produced something like this; it takes the grace of God.
I was thinking, as I was listening at the testimonies and hearing the great things that our blessed Lord has done with His people, and I was thinking how great it will be at that day. And I said to Brother … my good Brother Weston here… As he speaks each afternoon, it'll do you good to hear him; he certainly is a marvelous Bible teacher. And I said, "As I look down this tables and see the young and old seated together, there is one blessed assurance that I have, that I will eat with you again." It will not be a breakfast, maybe, but it will be a supper, when it's all over. I just wonder what we'll feel like then; if just a little touch, a foretaste of glory divine, brings this type of atmosphere, what will it be when we sit in the fullness of His anointing?
text_no:
Takk, Bror Carlson. Peter sa på pinsedagen: "Dette er det." Hvis dette ikke er det, vil jeg helst bli med dette til det kommer. Dette er fantastisk. Hvem skulle trodd at kristne forretningsmenn kunne produsere noe slikt; det krever Guds nåde.
Mens jeg lyttet til vitnesbyrdene og hørte om de store tingene som vår velsignede Herre har gjort for Sitt folk, tenkte jeg på hvor stort det vil være på den dagen. Jeg sa til Bror Weston her... Han taler hver ettermiddag, og det vil gjøre deg godt å høre ham; han er virkelig en enestående bibellærer. Jeg sa: "Når jeg ser nedover disse bordene og ser de unge og gamle sitte sammen, er det en velsignet forsikring jeg har, at jeg vil spise med dere igjen." Det vil kanskje ikke være en frokost, men en middag, når alt er over. Jeg lurer på hva vi vil føle da; hvis bare en liten berøring, en forsmak på den guddommelige herligheten, skaper en slik atmosfære, hvordan vil det være når vi sitter i fylden av Hans salvelse?

text:
I've often wondered what it would be, when the great tables was set across the skies, and the King of glory comes out, and looks down along that long table, and we sit just across from each other, just catch each other's hands and say, "You remember Tacoma that morning?"
Some old veteran sitting here with gray hair, that paved the road for me… I thought, the honor and the privilege it is to stand here this morning before such people and to try to speak to them, when many of you men were on the battlefield preaching, paving the road, when I was just a little sinner boy. I feel honored to stand here. And it isn't… I have very little to do in this; if there's any honor, it belongs to you. You stood on the street corners with a tambourine and a guitar, and you made the way. I'm just running over the road that you said would come. And God's grace has provided for us all. To Him we give praise and glory.
text_no:
Jeg har ofte lurt på hvordan det vil være når det store bordet er dekket over himmelens vidder og herlighetens Konge kommer ut, ser ned langs det lange bordet, og vi sitter rett overfor hverandre. Vi griper hverandres hender og sier: "Husker du Tacoma den morgenen?"
Noen gamle veteraner med grått hår, som banet veien for meg... Jeg tenkte på hvilken ære og hvilket privilegium det er å stå her denne morgenen foran slike mennesker og forsøke å tale til dere, når mange av dere menn var ute i feltet og forkynte, banet veien, mens jeg bare var en liten synder. Jeg føler meg beæret over å stå her. Og det er ikke... Jeg har svært lite å gjøre med dette; hvis det er noen ære, tilhører den dere. Dere sto på gatehjørnene med tamburin og gitar, og dere banet veien. Jeg løper bare på veien dere sa ville komme. Og Guds nåde har forsørget oss alle. Til Ham gir vi pris og ære.

text:
This Christian Businessmen's Fellowship has been lots to me. I was ordained in the Missionary Baptist Church by Dr. Roy E. Davis from Big Springs, Texas. And then I was … been a Baptist. You know the Baptist church, you don't be put out of a Baptist church for your doctrine, because they have no doctrine; it's a fellowship. It's a fellowship: the Baptist. And each church is sovereign in itself. What you're put out of a Baptist church for is immoral living. So, I left the Baptist church in order to be free from all denominations, that I could preach to the body of Christ.
I belong to one organization this morning. I have many honorary credentials at home, that people have sent me from different denominations, from the Presbyterian on down, and around. But I belong to one officially: that's the Christian Businessmen. And I have their card in my pocket, and I'm proud to say that I'm affiliated with this great group, because it is interdenominational. And I found such a wonderful bunch of men, though being just laymen, they are trying to do everything that's in their power to glorify Jesus Christ. And I'm certainly happy to be behind them with all that I have.
text_no:
Kristent Forretningsmannsfellesskap har betydd mye for meg. Jeg ble ordinert i den Misjonerende Baptistmenigheten av Dr. Roy E. Davis fra Big Springs, Texas. Deretter har jeg vært en Baptist. Du vet at i Baptistmenigheten blir du ikke ekskludert på grunn av din lære, fordi de ikke har noen fast lære; det er et fellesskap. Det er et fellesskap: Baptistene. Hver menighet er suveren i seg selv. Du blir ekskludert fra en Baptistmenighet på grunn av umoralsk livsførsel. Så jeg forlot Baptistmenigheten for å frigjøre meg fra alle konfesjoner, slik at jeg kunne forkynne for Kristi kropp.
I dag tilhører jeg én organisasjon. Jeg har mange æresbevisninger hjemme, sendt til meg fra ulike konfesjoner, fra Presbyterianske til forskjellige andre retninger. Men jeg tilhører én organisasjon offisielt: Kristent Forretningsmannsfellesskap. Jeg har deres medlemskort i lomma, og jeg er stolt av å være tilknyttet denne flotte gruppen, fordi den er interkonfesjonell. Jeg har møtt en fantastisk gjeng menn som, skjønt de bare er legfolk, gjør alt i deres makt for å ære Jesus Kristus. Jeg er veldig glad for å støtte dem med alt jeg har.

text:
When I first come into the evangelism, on the field in this type, I'd never heard of Pentecostals. Oh, I'd heard them say "a bunch of holy-rollers" and so forth. But I found them, they were one of the sweetest bunch of people, just as free as they could be, loving, good-hearted, good-natured. But I found among them (just like I did among the Baptist and the rest of them) differences between them. I could've taken one side like the Assemblies, or the Church of God, or so forth, but I stayed free so I could stay in the breach and say, "We're all brethren; let's just come together."
Jacob dug three wells. The first well, the Philistines run him away from it; I believe he called it "malice." And the second well he dug, they run him away from it; so he called them "strife." And he dug the third well and said, "There's room for us all."
text_no:
Da jeg først begynte med evangeliseringen på denne måten, hadde jeg aldri hørt om pinsevenner. Å ja, jeg hadde hørt folk kalle dem "en gjeng hellige-rullere" og så videre. Men jeg oppdaget at de var blant de hyggeligste menneskene: åpne, kjærlige, godhjertede og vennlige. Men jeg la merke til at det, akkurat som blant baptistene og de andre, fantes forskjeller mellom dem. Jeg kunne ha tatt parti for én gruppe som Assemblies eller Menigheten av Gud, men jeg valgte å forbli fri for å kunne stå i mellomrommet og si: "Vi er alle brødre; la oss bare komme sammen."
Jakob gravde tre brønner. Den første brønnen ble han jaget bort fra av filisterne, og jeg tror han kalte den "fiendskap." Den andre brønnen han gravde, ble han også jaget bort fra, og han kalte den "strid." Da han gravde den tredje brønnen, sa han: "Her er det plass til oss alle."

text:
So, I think them who are riding a one hump camel, two hump camels or three hump camels, we can all drink from this fountain; we can all drink together. And I'm so happy for this, and believing that someday that God will bring us all together, all people of all denominations, God's children, in one great ransomed church.
You know, Solomon's temple when it was cut out, it was cut out in different parts of the world. The trees, the cedars of Lebanon floated down to Joppa, and from there to ox-cart to Jerusalem, and so forth. And the stone masons, in the years of cutting the different stones, they shaped different ways. But when they come together, there wasn't a freak stone among them; every stone laid right to its place. And I think that's what God's a doing. He's just cutting us out of the Methodist, and the Baptist, and the Presbyterian, and the Pentecostals, but when Jesus comes we will all go stone to stone, cemented with the love of God; and God will take His church.
text_no:
Jeg tror at enten vi rir på enhumpede, tohumpede eller trehumpede kameler, kan vi alle drikke fra denne kilden; vi kan alle drikke sammen. Jeg er så glad for dette og tror at Gud en dag vil samle oss alle, alle mennesker fra alle konfesjoner, Guds barn, i én stor forløst menighet.
Som dere vet, ble Salomos tempel hugget ut på forskjellige steder i verden. Trærne, sedertrærne fra Libanon, ble fløtet ned til Joppa, og derfra med oksekjerrer til Jerusalem, og så videre. Steinhuggerne formet steinene på forskjellige måter i årenes løp. Men når de ble samlet, var det ikke en eneste uegnet stein; hver stein lå nøyaktig på sin plass. Jeg tror det er dette Gud gjør. Han hugger oss ut fra metodister, baptister, presbyterianere og pinsevenner, men når Jesus kommer, vil vi alle passe stein på stein, sammenføyd med Guds kjærlighet; og Gud vil ta Sitt folk.

text:
Now, it's so good to be in Tacoma. Tacoma's always had a place in my heart somehow; I don't know. But it's always been a real warm spot for Tacoma. And this northwest country here, beautiful, your trees and your … lots of water. Down on the desert in Phoenix not long ago, I was out on the desert, and everything there that you touch has a sticker on it. And you touch anything, it just … it's just lazy; it won't move. And it's got a sticker on it. Well, that same type of plant could be brought up here, and it will become a leaf, because it gets water.
And that's what I think about our churches. Many of them we think are cold, formal, indifferent; but they're just run out of spiritual water. All they need is a little water to soften them up, and they'll come out into a lovely leaf for the shade. And we just… God's got plenty of it. And you don't have to drink sparingly; He's the inexhaustible fountain of life.
text_no:
Det er så godt å være i Tacoma. Tacoma har alltid hatt en spesiell plass i hjertet mitt; jeg vet ikke helt hvorfor. Men jeg har alltid følt en varme for Tacoma og denne vakre nordvestre delen av landet, med trærne deres og alt vannet. Nede i ørkenen i Phoenix for ikke så lenge siden, var jeg ute i ørkenen, og alt der har torner. Alt du rører ved, er tregt og beveger seg ikke, og det har torner. Den samme plantearten kunne bli brakt hit, og den ville få blader på grunn av vannet.
Det er slik jeg ser på våre menigheter. Mange av dem oppfatter vi som kalde, formelle og likegyldige, men de har bare gått tom for åndelig vann. Alt de trenger er litt vann for å mykne opp, og de vil blomstre til en vakker løv for skygge. Gud har rikelig av dette vannet, og vi trenger ikke drikke sparsomt; Han er den uutømmelige livets kilde.

text:
Could you imagine: I've had people to tell me, "Brother Branham, I would ask God to heal me, but I know He's busy." My, could you imagine a little fish about that long, out in the middle of the Atlantic ocean, saying, "I'd better drink of this water sparingly; I may run out someday." That's just as intelligent of thinking that you could ever exhaust God's goodness to you.
Could you imagine a little mouse about that long under the great garners of Egypt saying, "I'd better just eat two grains a day; it may not last through the winter." Oh, my, you can't… He wants you to ask abundant, so that your joys could be full. Don't be afraid to ask God for anything, for He's willing, more willing to give it, than you are to receive it. If we just get that little shadow wiped away to know that He's willing to do it. You're His children; He loves you and He…
You know how you like minister and to do good things to your children, how much… That very strain of fatherhood, of parent to your children, comes from God (That's right.), because He's the Father of us all; and He loves us, and He wants to do for us. If He can just get us lined up with the blessings and so forth, He can trust us, then there's just… The heavens is open to all of us.
text_no:
Kan du forestille deg: Jeg har hatt folk som sier til meg, "Bror Branham, jeg vil be Gud om å helbrede meg, men jeg vet at Han er opptatt." Herlighet, kan du forestille deg en liten fisk på omtrent så stor, ute i Atlanterhavet, som sier, "Jeg bør drikke av dette vannet sparsomt, jeg kan gå tom en dag?" Det er like intelligent som å tro at du kunne uttømme Guds godhet mot deg.
Kan du forestille deg en liten mus på omtrent så stor under Egyptens store kornlagre si, "Jeg bør bare spise to korn om dagen; det kan hende at det ikke varer gjennom vinteren." Nei, Han vil at du skal be om rikelig, slik at din glede kan være full. Ikke vær redd for å be Gud om noe som helst, for Han er mer villig til å gi det enn du er til å motta det. Hvis vi bare kan fjerne den lille skyggen av tvil og innse at Han er villig til å gjøre det. Dere er Hans barn; Han elsker dere, og Han...
Du vet hvordan du liker å ta vare på og gjøre gode ting for dine barn, hvor mye... Den samme følelsen av farskap, foreldrekjærlighet til dine barn, kommer fra Gud (Det stemmer.), fordi Han er vår alles Far; og Han elsker oss, og Han ønsker å gjøre godt for oss. Hvis Han bare kan få oss i harmoni med velsignelsene, og så videre, at Han kan stole på oss, da er himmelen åpen for oss alle.

text:
Now, this morning I know, I… You've had so many good things, and I know we don't want to keep you too long; and I'm just a little hoarse from much speaking, and I don't want to take much of your time. But I would like to just go into this little subject this morning as a little drama. And then, it won't take too much voice, because I'm going to another eleven day meeting after … or a week (Sunday through Sunday) after this. And I'd like to read a portion of God's Word this morning out of the Bible. And just about the 19th chapter of Saint Luke we read this:
And Jesus entered and passed through Jericho.
And, behold, there was a man named Zacchaeus, which was the chief among the publicans, and he was rich.
And he sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not because of the press, because he was little of stature.
And he ran before, and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him: for he was to pass that way.
The Lord willing, I'd like to use my text right now for a few moments while you pray with me, that He Was To Pass That Way.
text_no:
I formiddag, jeg vet at dere har fått mange gode ting, og jeg ønsker ikke å holde dere for lenge. Jeg er litt hes etter mye tale, og jeg vil ikke ta for mye av deres tid. Men jeg vil gjerne gå inn i dette lille emnet som en liten drama denne morgenen. Det vil ikke kreve så mye stemmebruk, da jeg skal delta i et annet ellevte dags møte etter dette, eller en uke (søndag til søndag).
Jeg vil lese en del av Guds Ord denne morgenen fra Bibelen. Fra det 19. kapittelet i Evangeliet etter Lukas leser vi dette:
"Jesus kom inn i Jeriko og dro gjennom byen.
Der var en mann som het Sakkeus. Han var overtolleren og svært rik.
Han ville gjerne se hvem Jesus var, men han kunne ikke komme til for folkemengden, for han var liten av vekst.
Da løp han i forvegen og klatret opp i et morbærtre for å se Ham, siden Jesus skulle komme forbi der."
Om Herren vil, vil jeg bruke min tekst i noen øyeblikk mens dere ber med meg, at "Han Skulle Komme Forbi Der".

text:
It must have been an awful night; the little fellow was so weary; he was tossed about and… You know those sleepless nights… And his wife, Rebekah, said, "Zacchaeus, what's wrong with you?"
"Oh," he said, "nothing, perhaps maybe the business in the city was so bad today, and that's what's holding me, the way it is tonight; I can't sleep."
But you know, Rebekah knowed better than that. She had been a believer on the Lord Jesus, and she'd been praying for her husband, a businessman of the city, to become a follower of the Lord Jesus. And you know, conviction can make you very restless; it does that. And so, a good praying wife or mother can do more for the kingdom of God on her knees at home, than sometimes the preacher can do on the platform.
text_no:
Det må ha vært en forferdelig natt; den lille gutten var så sliten, han ble kastet rundt og... Du vet, slike søvnløse netter... Og hans kone, Rebekka, sa: "Sakkeus, hva er galt med deg?"
"Åh," sa han, "ingenting, kanskje forretningene i byen gikk dårlig i dag, og det er det som holder meg våken i natt; jeg får ikke sove."
Men Rebekka visste bedre. Hun hadde vært en troende på Herren Jesus og bedt for at hennes mann, en forretningsmann i byen, skulle bli en etterfølger av Herren Jesus. Du vet, overbevisning kan gjøre deg veldig urolig; det er slik det fungerer. En god, bedende kone eller mor kan gjøre mer for Guds rike på knærne hjemme enn noen ganger forkynneren kan gjøre fra plattformen.

text:
So, she knew that Jesus of Nazareth was coming to the city the next day, because she was a follower of Him; she loved Him. And she'd found something in Him that was different from other men. And no one can ever come in contact with this blessed Lord Jesus, but what knows that He's just different from everyone else. When the Romans come to hear Him, they returned back and said, "Never a man spake like this." There was something different when you meet Jesus; it changes your life.
And she thought if she could ever get Zacchaeus to go down and to meet Jesus, that would be sufficient.
But in them days they were very critical of Jesus, just like they are mostly today. They criticize Him. And so, the popular belief of Jesus was, He was a fanatic; He was going around disagreeing with the priests and trying to tear up their religion. And they had everything just so settled down, till they didn't want that tore up; that's just the way they wanted it. But you know, sometimes God has different ideas.
text_no:
Hun visste at Jesus fra Nasaret skulle komme til byen neste dag, ettersom hun var en etterfølger av Ham og elsket Ham. Hun hadde funnet noe i Ham som var annerledes fra andre menn. Ingen kan komme i kontakt med denne velsignede Herren Jesus uten å merke at Han er forskjellig fra alle andre. Da romerne hørte Ham, kom de tilbake og sa: "Aldri har noe menneske talt slik som denne mannen." Det er noe spesielt når man møter Jesus; det endrer livet ditt.
Hun tenkte at hvis hun bare kunne få Sakkeus til å møte Jesus, ville det være nok. På den tiden var folk veldig kritiske til Jesus, akkurat som mange er i dag. De kritiserte Ham. Den vanlige oppfatningen var at Jesus var en fanatiker som gikk rundt og var uenig med prestene og prøvde å ødelegge deres religion. De hadde strukturen sin på plass og ville ikke at den skulle forandres. Men noen ganger har Gud andre planer.

text:
So, she knew that if she could ever get Zacchaeus, knowing that he was an honest hearted man… Now, he was a businessman of Jericho, very rich man. And let's say he owned a restaurant. But he was very rich, but he had not done anything wrong to be rich; he was honest. And no matter what business you're in, if you're honest, God can use you. An honest heart will always answer to truth.
So, Rebekah knew this, and she'd been praying much for little Zacchaeus, knowing that Jesus had promised to visit the city. Of course, it always brings out a mixed multitude, there'd be some there to criticize Him, and some would make fun, and others would believe. But if she could only get Zacchaeus to see Him. But you know, the priest had said … rabbi had said, "If anyone goes to hear this man, they get their papers. They'll be excommunicated." Or, "We don't tolerate such stuff as that. And He's just coming here to the city just to cause trouble, to break up our churches, and get people all stirred up, so no one must go see Him."
text_no:
Rebekka visste at hvis hun kunne få Zacchaeus, en mann med ærlig hjerte, til å møte Jesus ... Han var en velstående forretningsmann i Jeriko. La oss si at han eide en restaurant. Han var rik, men hadde ikke gjort noe galt for å oppnå sin rikdom; han var ærlig. Uansett hvilken virksomhet du driver med, hvis du er ærlig, kan Gud bruke deg. Et ærlig hjerte vil alltid svare på sannheten.
Rebekka var klar over dette og hadde bedt mye for lille Zacchaeus, vel vitende om at Jesus hadde lovet å besøke byen. Et slikt besøk tiltrakk seg alltid en blandet mengde; noen kom for å kritisere, andre for å gjøre narr, og noen for å tro. Men hvis hun bare kunne få Zacchaeus til å møte Ham. Prestene hadde nemlig sagt, "Hvis noen går for å høre denne mannen, mister de sine papirer. De vil bli ekskommunisert." Eller, "Vi tolererer ikke slikt. Han kommer bare til byen for å skape trøbbel, bryte opp våre menigheter, og få folk til å bli opprørte, så ingen må dra for å se Ham."

text:
So Zacchaeus, being a charter member of the great church and a bosom friend (He played golf with the rabbi.), and, why, he just couldn't afford to go out there, you know. So, there's only one thing that could change that situation; that was prayer. Prayer changes things. It just does something when you pray. So, you can see why he had a restless night: Rebekah was praying; God was listening.
Little Zacchaeus couldn't rest. Oh, he was so torn up. So finally, Rebekah kind of turned over on her side, and she said, "Thank You, Lord, I know You're dealing with him, for he's getting restless." [Tape sputters.] Businessman, see. When he gets restless, just remember, God's a dealing. So, she turned [Tape sputters.] … to find the very garment he had on. And he was grooming his hair before the glass, you know. She said, "Zacchaeus, why, it's early, what are you doing up so early this morning?" [Tape interrupted with noises.]
He said, "Dear, I couldn't sleep. I thought I would just go out and get some air, before I go to the work this morning." She knew God was dealing with him, getting up early.
text_no:
Sakkeus, som var et grunnleggende medlem av den store menigheten og en nær venn (han spilte golf med rabbineren), kunne ikke tillate seg å gå ut der. Det var bare én ting som kunne endre situasjonen: bønn. Bønn forandrer ting. Den gjør noe når du ber. Så du kan forstå hvorfor han hadde en urolig natt: Rebekka ba, og Gud lyttet.
Lille Sakkeus fikk ikke sove. Han var så opprørt. Til slutt snudde Rebekka seg over på siden og sa: "Takk, Herre, jeg vet at Du arbeider med ham, for han blir rastløs." Forretningsmannen Sakkeus, se. Når han blir rastløs, husk at Gud arbeider. Så snudde hun seg og fant det samme plagget han hadde på seg. Han stelte håret sitt foran speilet, vet du. Hun sa: "Sakkeus, det er tidlig, hva gjør du oppe så tidlig i dag?"
Han svarte: "Kjære, jeg kunne ikke sove. Jeg tenkte jeg skulle gå ut og få litt luft før jeg drar på jobb i dag." Hun visste at Gud arbeidet med ham da han sto opp så tidlig.

text:
So he grooms himself all up, and she watched him as he went down the steps, and walked out to the street, and standing with his hands behind him. And as he stood there, let's see what he's thinking. He said, "You know, this is the day that that Galilean prophet is coming to the city. I would like to get a good look at that guy. You know, I've heard the pro and con, but if I can ever see Him, I'm going to walk right up in His face and tell Him what I think about Him. Oh, yes, sir. I'm going to make Him feel little when I see Him. And I know now that He's supposed to come through the south gate, so I'll go down there at the south gate, and I'll wait. And when He comes through, I'm going to give Him a piece of my mind." You know, we can get that way, you know.
So, he said, "I'll stand right down there and wait till this holy-roller Prophet comes through, and I'll let Him know that all of His divine healing, and all of this stuff is nonsense. Because rabbi went through the high schools; he knows what he's talking about. So, I'll tell Him. Now, He might be able to influence a bunch of illiterates with His mental telepathy, but He certainly can't speak to a man that's educated like me; I'll be able to tell Him about it." Well, God… [Blank spot on tape.]
text_no:
Han steller seg nøye, og hun overvåker ham mens han går ned trappen og ut på gaten, hvor han står med hendene bak ryggen. La oss se hva han tenker. Han sier til seg selv: "Dette er dagen da den galileiske profeten kommer til byen. Jeg vil gjerne få et godt blikk på den fyren. Jeg har hørt mye for og imot, men hvis jeg får se Ham, skal jeg gå rett opp til Ham og si hva jeg mener om Ham. Ja, absolutt. Jeg skal få Ham til å føle seg liten når jeg møter Ham. Jeg vet at Han skal komme gjennom sørporten, så jeg går dit og venter. Når Han kommer gjennom, skal jeg gi Ham en bit av min mening." Vi kan bli slik, vet du.
Så han sier: "Jeg skal stå der og vente til denne helligdoms-profeten kommer gjennom, og jeg skal la Ham få vite at all Hans helbredelse og alt dette tullet er nonsens. For rabbineren har gått gjennom høyskolene; han vet hva han snakker om. Så jeg skal fortelle Ham. Nå kan Han kanskje påvirke en haug av analfabeter med Sin mentale telepati, men Han kan absolutt ikke snakke til en utdannet mann som meg; jeg skal fortelle Ham hvordan det er." Vel, Gud... [Tomt område på lydbånd.]

text:
… morning when I was thinking, when I've went into the restaurants to eat and some little silly teen-ager come in and dropped in a nickel to this old booglie-wooglie or ever-what that stuff is, and I just have to almost throw my ears in low gear to not hear it, so that you can eat. And this morning while we were eating, that beautiful song of "Onward Christian Soldiers marching as to war." Oh, how that thrilled my heart; my meal just tasted different. Because we're in heavenly atmosphere. And where Jesus is it's always heavenly.
And then, while they were waiting, he was pushing and crowding and he said, "Now, just a moment, if I stand here, I'm so little, that somebody will push before me, and… Well, I might as well go back home." But you know, if you've purposed in your heart to see Jesus, you'll not be defeated. That's right.
text_no:
... morgen satt jeg og tenkte på når jeg har gått inn på restauranter for å spise, og en liten fjollete tenåring kommer inn og slipper en mynt i denne gamle booglie-wooglie-maskinen eller hva det nå er. Jeg må nesten lukke ørene helt for å ikke høre det, slik at jeg kan spise. Men denne morgenen, mens vi spiste, ble den vakre sangen "Onward Christian Soldiers marching as to war" spilt. Å, hvordan det gledet mitt hjerte; måltidet smakte bare annerledes. Fordi vi er i himmelsk atmosfære. Og der Jesus er, er det alltid himmelsk.
Og så, mens de ventet, presset og trengte han seg frem og sa: "Nå, bare et øyeblikk, hvis jeg står her, er jeg så liten at noen vil presse seg foran meg, og ... Vel, jeg kan like gjerne gå hjem." Men du vet, hvis du har bestemt deg i hjertet ditt for å se Jesus, vil du ikke bli beseiret. Det er riktig.

text:
Well, he puts his hands behind him, start walking back, something said to him, "Now, are you going to go away without telling Him what you said you were going to tell Him?"
And he said to himself, "No, I will not be defeated." So he purposed in his heart: he's going to wait till he sees Him. And then, after he sees Him, he's going to be satisfied whether he's right or wrong.
So he said, "You know I heard Rebekah saying that He was going over to Levinski's Restaurant. Well, why would He go over there? Now, you know I have the best restaurant in this town, and so why would He eat over there instead of eating at my restaurant? Mine should be the best. After all, my … I'm a great charter member up here of the great Sanhedrin, and I'm a notable man in this city. And I'm an intelligent man, and I have the best of foods. But why would He go up there to eat with them?" It was because He was invited up there to eat with them. Jesus goes where He's invited. That's right. He will never push Himself on you; you've got to invite Him. That's true.
text_no:
Han legger hendene bak ryggen og begynner å gå, da noe sier til ham: "Nå, skal du dra uten å fortelle Ham det du sa du skulle fortelle Ham?"
Han sier til seg selv: "Nei, jeg skal ikke bli beseiret." Han bestemmer seg for at han skal vente til han ser Ham. Når han ser Ham, skal han være fornøyd, enten han har rett eller feil.
Han sier: "Jeg hørte Rebekka si at Han skulle til Levinskis Restaurant. Hvorfor skulle Han dra dit? Jeg har den beste restauranten i byen, så hvorfor skulle Han spise der i stedet for hos meg? Min restaurant burde være den beste. Jeg er tross alt en fremtredende chartermedlem av det store Sanhedrin, og jeg er en kjent mann i denne byen. Jeg er en intelligent mann, og jeg har den beste maten. Men hvorfor går Han dit for å spise?"
Det er fordi Han ble invitert dit. Jesus går dit Han blir invitert. Det er riktig. Han vil aldri trenge seg på; du må invitere Ham. Det er sannheten.

text:
So, he says, "I know if He goes up there, He has to go down here to a certain way to Hallelujah Avenue. So, as He passes by Hallelujah Avenue, when He turns the corner to head up to the restaurant, I'm going to wait right there when He makes that bend, and I'll be all by myself. I'll get a good look at Him, and I will know, because I have the intelligence. So I will know just what that fellow is when I see Him." That's right. You will.
So, he goes up there to the corner, and he stands at the corner, and he says, "Now, here I stand. Now, I'm going to wait till He comes around that corner there, and then I'm going to get a good view of Him. And I'll know all about this fellow. When I stand up in my class next Sabbath morning, I'll tell them about this Galilean Prophet; just wait till I see Him."
text_no:
Han sier: "Jeg vet at når Han kommer dit opp, må Han gå en bestemt vei til Halleluja-avenyen. Når Han passerer Halleluja-avenyen og tar svingen opp mot restauranten, vil jeg vente der Han svinger. Da vil jeg være alene og få en god titt på Ham. Jeg vil vite, for jeg har intelligensen. Så jeg vil vite akkurat hvem denne fyren er når jeg ser Ham." Det stemmer, det vil du.
Han går til hjørnet og stiller seg opp, og sier: "Nå står jeg her. Jeg vil vente til Han kommer rundt hjørnet, så jeg kan få et godt blikk på Ham. Da vil jeg vite alt om denne mannen. Når jeg står opp i klassen min neste sabbatsmorgen, skal jeg fortelle dem om denne galileiske profeten. Bare vent til jeg ser Ham."

text:
So, he stood on the corner patting his little foot, you know, and his face red, because he'd been pushed out there; he thought he was somebody. As long as you think you're somebody, you're going to get nowhere. You just remember that. You got to get away from what you are. We're not much anyhow.
Here sometime ago I was down in Tennessee. I was in a museum, and they had the estimations there of the human body. And a man that weighs a hundred and fifty pounds, in chemicals is worth eighty-four cents. So, there was two boys standing there talking about it. The human body, you got just enough calcium for so-and-so, and enough whitewash to sprinkle a hen's nest, I guess, or something like that in your body. And then you'll put a ten dollar hat on it, a fifty dollar coat on that eighty-four cents, and stick your nose up (If it'd rain, it'd drown you.), and think you're somebody. Now, that's right. That's the way the human race acts. Now, isn't that right? That's right. Somebody, and you're eighty-four cents. That's right.
One boy said to the other one; he said, "John, we're not very much after all, are we? Eighty-four cents."
I touched the boys; I said, "But, boys, you've got a soul that's worth ten million worlds; take care of that. The eighty-four cents don't amount to very much, but the ten thousands worlds means everything to you."
LUKE19:3
text_no:
Han sto på hjørnet og trippet med foten, og ansiktet var rødt fordi han hadde blitt dyttet dit; han trodde han var noe spesielt. Så lenge du tror du er noe spesielt, kommer du ingen vei. Husk det. Du må komme bort fra det du er. Vi er ikke mye uansett.
For en tid tilbake var jeg i Tennessee. Jeg var i et museum, og de hadde beregninger der av menneskekroppen. En mann som veier syttifem kilo, er i kjemikalier verdt åttifire øre. Det sto to gutter der og snakket om det. Menneskekroppen inneholder bare nok kalsium til å dekke så-og-så, og nok kalk til å strø en hønserede, antar jeg. Så setter du en hatt til ti dollar på den, en frakk til femti dollar på de åttifire ørene, og hever nesen (hvis det regner, ville du drukne), og tror du er noe spesielt. Det er slik menneskeheten oppfører seg. Er det ikke sant? Jo, det er det. Noen tror de er noe, og de er verdt åttifire øre. Det er riktig.
En av guttene sa til den andre: "John, vi er ikke mye likevel, er vi? Åttifire øre." Jeg henvendte meg til guttene og sa: "Men gutter, dere har en sjel som er verdt ti millioner verdener. Ta vare på den. De åttifire ørene betyr ikke mye, men de ti millioner verdener betyr alt for dere."

text:
So, as this little fellow stood there, he begin to think, "You know what? Being as small as I am, and if that same crowd that was down there… If I'm told right, one glimpse of Him, they all follow Him after that." That's right, just once come in contact with Him, you'll go everywhere He goes from then on. So he said, "If they come up here, I'm too little. The same crowd down there, plus all it'll pick up between here and there will be here, so then, I won't be any better off, standing right here than I was standing down there."
So, he begin to think, "Now, what could I do?" And if you're determined to see Jesus, God will make a way for you to see Him. So he begin to think, "What could he do?" So standing by his side was a sycamore tree. He thought, "That's a good idea. I'll just get up in that sycamore tree. When I get up there, I'll just get me a good place and sit down; and when He passes by, I'll be all by myself, and I can see Him good."
text_no:
Mens denne lille gutten sto der, begynte han å tenke: "Vet du hva? Siden jeg er så liten, og hvis den samme folkemengden som var der nede... Hvis jeg har hørt riktig, så følger de alle Ham etter bare å ha sett Ham én gang." Det stemmer; bare én gang i kontakt med Ham, og du vil følge Ham overalt der Han går. Så han sa, "Hvis de kommer opp hit, er jeg for liten. Den samme mengden der nede, pluss alle de vil samle underveis, vil være her, så da vil jeg ikke være noe bedre stilt her enn jeg var der nede."
Så begynte han å tenke: "Hva kunne jeg gjøre?" Hvis du er fast bestemt på å se Jesus, vil Gud finne en vei for deg. Så han begynte å tenke: "Hva kunne han gjøre?" Ved siden av ham sto et morbærtre. Han tenkte: "Det er en god idé. Jeg skal bare klatre opp i det morbærtreet. Når jeg kommer opp dit, skal jeg finne meg et godt sted å sitte ned, og når Han går forbi, vil jeg være helt alene og kunne se Ham godt."

text:
But now, the next thing is: how's he going to get up there; he's little? And he had on his best garments; he was groomed to his best. You see, the world likes glamour. Jesus wasn't glamorous; He's just a plain, common, man. And the Gospel today is not glamour; it's just plain old-fashioned salvation to all men. He hasn't changed; He's the same.
But the world thinks you have to be dressed so-and-so. You have to put on your best manners. You have to be something. As Congressman Upshaw used to say, "Be something that you ain't." And that's right. There's so much put-on among people today, trying to be something, try to talk different, try to … something that you're not. I pray that I will live to see the day when men will be what they are. Then every man will know how to take you. God speed the day, that when every man that claims to be a Christian will be just what he is. If I was, didn't believe Christ, I'd be against Him; I'd be here speaking against Him. But I believe Him, and I love Him, and I'm for Him; with everything that's in me, I'm for Him.
text_no:
Men nå kommer det neste: Hvordan skal han komme seg opp der når han er så liten? Og han hadde på seg sine beste klær, stiligst stelt. Du ser, verden liker glamour. Jesus var ikke glamorøs; Han var bare en enkel, vanlig mann. Og Evangeliet i dag er ikke glamorøst; det er bare gammelmodig frelse for alle mennesker. Han har ikke forandret Seg; Han er den samme.
Men verden tror at du må kle deg på en bestemt måte. Du må vise de beste manerene dine. Du må være noe spesielt. Som Kongressmedlem Upshaw pleide å si, "Vær noe du ikke er." Og det er riktig. Det er så mye falskhet blant folk i dag, de prøver å være noe annet, å snakke annerledes, å være noe de ikke er. Jeg ber om at jeg får oppleve dagen når menn vil være det de er. Da vil enhver vite hvordan han skal forholde seg til deg. Måtte Gud fremskynde den dagen når hver mann som hevder å være kristen, bare er det han er. Hvis jeg ikke trodde på Kristus, ville jeg være mot Ham; jeg ville stå her og tale imot Ham. Men jeg tror på Ham, jeg elsker Ham, og jeg er for Ham; med alt som er i meg, er jeg for Ham.

text:
So, Zacchaeus begin to wonder how he would ever be able to see this Prophet as He passed. And remember, at home all the time in the house, was Rebekah still praying. "Oh, God, in some way, make a way that Zacchaeus will come in contact with this Prophet today. For he's a good man; he's honest. And I believe if You would ever let him contact this Prophet of Galilee, I believe he would become a devout Christian. So, I'm praying, Lord, that You'll help my poor beloved husband."
God knows we need more women like that today. When womanhood is broke, the backbone of the nation is broke. When women get to a place that our American women are today, it's a disgrace the way our American women act. I got a clipping out of the paper, that I believe, it was about sixty percent of the boys that went overseas in this last war, was divorced from their wives before they come back: their wives running off with somebody else in these defense plants, working. She has got no business in a defense plant. A woman's place is at home with her children. And when she… Now if her husband's sick and she has to work, that's different. But woman's place is at home in the kitchen; and when she leaves that, she's out of her place. Exactly right.
text_no:
Så, Sakkeus begynte å lure på hvordan han skulle få se denne Profeten når Han passerte. Og husk, hjemme i huset, satt Rebekka hele tiden og ba. "Å, Gud, lag en vei slik at Sakkeus kan komme i kontakt med denne Profeten i dag. For han er en god mann; han er ærlig. Og jeg tror at hvis Du lar ham møte denne Profeten fra Galilea, vil han bli en hengiven kristen. Så jeg ber, Herre, om at Du vil hjelpe min kjære ektemann."
Gud vet at vi trenger flere slike kvinner i dag. Når kvinnens posisjon er brutt, er ryggraden i nasjonen brutt. Når kvinner kommer til det punktet hvor våre amerikanske kvinner er i dag, er det en skam måten de oppfører seg på. Jeg har en utklipp fra avisen som sier at omtrent seksti prosent av guttene som reiste utenlands i den siste krigen, ble skilt før de kom tilbake: deres koner løp av med noen andre i disse forsvarsverkene der de jobbet. Hun har ikke noe å gjøre i en forsvarsvirksomhet. En kvinnes plass er hjemme med barna sine. Og hvis mannen hennes er syk og hun må jobbe, er det annerledes. Men en kvinnes plass er på kjøkkenet; og når hun forlater det, er hun ute av sin plass. Helt riktig.

text:
And when women get to smoking cigarettes, as they're doing today, and even women that call themselves Christians, smoking cigarettes… It's the lowest thing that I know that a woman can do, is to smoke cigarettes. That's right. I have no apologies for that remark. For if this Angel of the Lord … if you judge Him to be the Lord, your chances will be thin when you come to the judgment bar smoking cigarettes. That's right. There's no use of it. And it's the greatest sabotage; it's the greatest fifth columnist plan that's on the earth. Eighty percent of the mothers that give birth to babies in this United States---cigarette smoking mothers---they can't raise their babies as they should to their breasts; they have to give them the bottle, because they take nicotine poison and never reach eighteen months of age.
It ain't Russia that's going to hurt us; our morals are rottening ourselves. It isn't the robin that pecks on the apple that hurts it; it's the worm at the core. America is defeated by her own immorals. That's right. And when motherhood is broke, the backbone of the nation is broke.
text_no:
Når kvinner begynner å røyke sigaretter, slik de gjør i dag, inkludert kvinner som kaller seg kristne, er det noe av det laveste en kvinne kan gjøre. Det stemmer. Jeg beklager ikke den uttalelsen. For hvis denne Herrens Engel … hvis du bedømmer Ham til å være Herren, vil dine sjanser være små når du står foran dommens stol med en sigarett i hånden. Det er riktig. Det er ingen grunn til det. Og det er den største formen for sabotasje og skjult undergraving på jorden. Åtti prosent av mødre som føder barn i USA---røykende mødre---kan ikke amme barna sine som de burde; de må gi dem flaske, fordi de tar inn nikotin som forgifter barna før de når atten måneders alder.
Det er ikke Russland som vil skade oss; det er vår egen moral som råtner. Det er ikke fuglen som hakker på eplet som skader det; det er ormen i kjernen. Amerika blir beseiret av sin egen umoral. Det stemmer. Når morsrollen er ødelagt, er nasjonens ryggrad ødelagt.

text:
When I was in Hottentot Africa, someone said to me, "Brother Branham, don't you all have any nice women over in America? All your songs … there's something dirty about your women." I just had to bow my head and walk away. It's the worst in the world.
Oh, and you idolize these idols of these Elvis Presleys and so forth, and permit that stuff into your house. Throw that trash out of your house. We are Christians. Buzzards eat on dead carcass, and we're not buzzards; we're doves. I'm preaching on that tonight. All right. So if your desires in your heart is right, and your appetite's right, you'll eat and do the right thing; it's in your heart to do it.
text_no:
Da jeg var i Hottentot i Afrika, sa noen til meg: "Bror Branham, har dere ikke noen hyggelige kvinner i Amerika? Alle sangene deres … det er noe skittent med kvinnene deres." Jeg måtte bare bøye hodet og gå bort. Det er det verste i verden.
Å, og dere forguder disse idolene som Elvis Presley og lignende, og tillater det inn i hjemmene deres. Kast det søppelet ut av husene deres. Vi er kristne. Åtselsdyr spiser på døde kadavre, og vi er ikke åtselsdyr; vi er duer. Jeg forkynner om dette i kveld. Så hvis begjæret i hjertet ditt er riktig, og appetitten riktig, vil du gjøre det rette; det er i hjertet ditt å gjøre det.

text:
Now, and the world wants glamour. They don't want the old-fashioned preacher anymore, the old hellfire and brimstone, as we used to preach it. Today they want a little curly-headed, Hollywood type, with a little frocked-tailed coat on, and I don't know what, and stand up and crack a few jokes. We're got too many jokes on the public today, the Arthur Godfreys and so forth, and all these little (what you call thems), of that. But what we need today is the old-fashioned Gospel in the power and the resurrection of Jesus Christ, and a clean-up in the church from the pulpit to the cloak room. That's right. We need it, men and women. We got too much Hollywood evangelism, a lot of acting and pretending. We need the real genuine Jesus Christ. Amen.
So, that glamour doesn't take with Jesus. All the old fine robe that he had on, his fine groomed hair, and his fine Egyptian-made robe would never attract the attention of Jesus Christ. He don't care how poor you're dressed, or how you're otherwise. It's your heart that He's looking at.
text_no:
I dag ønsker verden glamour. De vil ikke ha de gammeldagse forkynnerne lenger, de som talte om helvete og svovel som vi pleide å gjøre. Nå vil de ha en liten, krøllete Hollywood-type i en fin kjolefrakk, som står der og forteller noen vitser. Vi har for mange vitser i offentligheten i dag, med Arthur Godfrey og så videre, og alle disse små (hva du kaller dem). Men det vi trenger i dag er det gammeldagse Evangeliet med kraften og oppstandelsen til Jesus Kristus, og en opprydding i menigheten fra prekestolen til garderoben. Det er sant. Vi trenger det, menn og kvinner. Vi har for mye Hollywood-evangelisme, mye skuespill og forstillelse. Vi trenger den ekte og sanne Jesus Kristus. Amen.
Denne glamouren imponerer ikke Jesus. Alle de fine kappene han hadde på, det velstelte håret og den fine, egyptisklagde kappen, ville aldri tiltrekke seg oppmerksomheten til Jesus Kristus. Han bryr Seg ikke om hvor fattigslig du er kledd, eller hvordan du ellers er. Det er ditt hjerte Han ser på.

text:
So, Zacchaeus thought, "Now, I cannot climb up that tree. If I would, and I'd have to come down and meet Him, I would not be presentable."
Here some time ago in my church, there was a girl that I asked her to sing a special that night. And she was a wonderful little singer, and she was out of a poor home, real poor; and they had a hard way of going. And she said she could not sing. And I asked her why. She wouldn't tell me why. And she went out; she told another girl that she didn't have the money to put a manicure in her hair. You know, one of them curly things. And so she said that she… (That's wrong, I know that; that's not right. What is it? Anyhow, it's a permanent … permanent in her hair. What is a manicure anyhow? Oh, fingernails. Oh, my.) She didn't have the money to put a permanent in her hair. And I told that girl; I said, "You know what, I wouldn't let you sing now." That's right. You're not getting up there to show off, or to be a show before those people; you come to sing the Gospel of Jesus Christ. That's right. Any other attitude from that, God would refuse it. True.
text_no:
Zakkeus tenkte: "Jeg kan ikke klatre opp i det treet. Om jeg gjorde det og måtte klatre ned igjen for å møte Ham, ville jeg ikke være presentabel."
For en tid tilbake var det en jente i menigheten min som jeg ba om å synge en spesiell sang den kvelden. Hun var en fantastisk liten sanger, men kom fra et svært fattig hjem, og de hadde det veldig tøft. Hun sa at hun ikke kunne synge, men ville ikke fortelle meg hvorfor. Senere fortalte hun en annen jente at hun ikke hadde råd til å ta en permanent i håret. 
Jeg fortalte henne at det ikke var riktig. Jeg sa: "Du skjønner, nå vil jeg ikke la deg synge." Det er riktig. Du skal ikke stå der oppe for å vise deg frem; du kommer for å synge Evangeliet om Jesus Kristus. Ethvert annet motiv ville Gud avvise. Det er sant.

text:
So, Zacchaeus, his little robe wouldn't make any difference. But if you're determined to see Jesus, God will make a way for you to see Him. So, he said, "I cannot get into this tree now. So, there must be some way." And God always has a way. So, he happened to look down at the corner, and the city garbage can was sitting there. "Well," he thought, "perhaps, maybe I'll go down, and get the garbage can, and pull it up here; I can get on the garbage can, and then shinny up the tree, and get up there all right." So, he thought that was a very good idea. You know, God knows how to take us off of the high horse.
So, he goes down to the corner, and he takes a hold of the garbage can, and the city collector had not yet come by to take his deposit; so there was … every can was full. There was nothing else in sight, so he thought, "Well, being all… He heard coming down around the bend… "I'll have to hurry, because He's not very far down; I hear a great noise."
You know, I kind of like that. Everywhere you found Jesus, you found a little noise. That kind of fits my Irish disposition, and especially when it's been changed.
text_no:
Selv om den lille kappen til Sakkeus ikke spilte noen rolle, hvis du er bestemt på å se Jesus, vil Gud finne en måte for deg å se Ham. Han sa til seg selv: "Jeg kan ikke klatre i dette treet nå, men det må finnes en annen måte." Og Gud har alltid en løsning. Han fikk øye på en søppelbøtte på hjørnet. "Kanskje," tenkte han, "jeg kan gå ned, ta søppelbøtten, sette den her; jeg kan klatre opp på søppelbøtten og deretter klatre opp i treet." Dette syntes han var en god idé. Gud vet hvordan Han skal bringe oss ned på jorden.
Så han gikk ned til hjørnet og grep tak i søppelbøtten. Renovasjonsbilen hadde ennå ikke hentet søppelet, så alle bøttene var fulle. Det var ingen annen løsning i sikte, så han tenkte: "Jeg må skynde meg, for Han er ikke langt unna; jeg hører en stor larm."
Jeg liker det. Overalt hvor du fant Jesus, fant du også litt støy. Det passer godt med min irske legning, spesielt når den har blitt forandret.

text:
Then, you know, in the temple when Aaron the priest was anointed to go into the holiest of holies, he had on the hems of his garment a pomegranate and a bell. And the only way they could tell that he had not been killed in the presence of God, that he was still alive, was that noise that that walking made; they knowed he was still alive. And I think sometime when I hear a little "Amen," or "Praise the Lord," there's still a little life somewhere, just a little noise from somewhere.
So, little Zacchaeus tried to pull the garbage can, and he couldn't do it. So, he said, "I'll have to hurry." So, he reaches down with his arms, because he was determined to see Jesus now; and he takes a hold of the garbage can; new garment on. Here he comes, walking with the garbage can. And he hears somebody laugh, and he looks, and it's his competitor standing on the corner, said, "I wish you would look; Zacchaeus has got him a new job. Well, the fine Zacchaeus, restaurant man, has become the city garbage collector."
text_no:
I tempelet, da Aron, presten, ble salvet for å gå inn i det aller helligste, hadde han granatepler og bjeller på sømmene av kappen sin. Den eneste måten de kunne vite at han ikke var drept i Guds nærvær, men fortsatt i live, var lyden han laget når han gikk. Denne lyden bekreftet at han var i live. Når jeg noen ganger hører et lite "Amen" eller "Pris Herren," tenker jeg på at det fortsatt er litt liv et sted, bare en liten lyd fra et sted.
Lille Sakkeus prøvde å flytte søppelbøtta, men klarte det ikke. "Jeg må skynde meg," tenkte han. Han var fast bestemt på å se Jesus, så han bøyde seg ned, tok tak i søppelbøtta med den nye kappen sin på, og begynte å gå med den. Han hørte noen le, og da han så opp, var det konkurrenten hans som sto på hjørnet og lo. "Se, Sakkeus har fått seg en ny jobb. Restaurantinnehaveren Sakkeus har blitt byens søppelmann."

text:
Oh, his little face got red; he looked around. And you know, God just makes coming to Jesus so humble sometimes it makes your face turn red. He makes you do things that you think you wouldn't do. But if you're determined, you'll do it.
So, when he got there, you know, his righteous indignation begin to rise. He couldn't deny it then. You know, like people sometime saying, "Well, I'll go down; I'll find out; I'll sit way back at the back." But when you're sitting there, there happens to be somebody that you criticized the meetings to, happens to be sitting over, says, "How do you do?"
"Oh, I… My… That'll hurt my business as sure as the world, humph," see. But you're done caught. So, that's the way you are this morning; you're caught now; you're right among them. Here you sit.
So, their face got red, you know, and looked around; he said, "You know… Well, let it go anyhow. I'm determined to see it through." That's the way: Be determined to see it through. God's put it on your heart to watch this Jesus of Nazareth to see if it's the truth, to see if this great move of God comes from God, open up your Bible and stay with it; that's the way to do it.
text_no:
Hans ansikt ble rødt, og han så seg rundt. Du vet, Gud gjør det noen ganger så ydmykende å komme til Jesus at ansiktet ditt kan rødme. Han får deg til å gjøre ting du trodde du ikke ville gjøre. Men hvis du er bestemt, vil du gjøre det.
Da han kom dit, steg hans rettferdige harme. Han kunne ikke fornekte det da. Som når folk noen ganger sier: "Vel, jeg skal gå ned; jeg skal finne ut av det; jeg skal sitte helt bakerst." Men når du sitter der, er det tilfeldigvis noen der som du kritiserte møtene til. Så sier de: "Hvordan går det?"
"Oh, nei... Det vil ødelegge forretningen min, det er sikkert," ser du. Men da er du fanget. Akkurat slik er det i dag; du er fanget nå, du er midt blant dem. Her sitter du.
Så, ansiktet ble rødt, og han så seg rundt; han sa, "Du vet... Vel, la det gå uansett. Jeg er bestemt på å se det gjennom." Det er slik det skal være: Vær bestemt på å se det gjennom. Gud har lagt det på hjertet ditt å undersøke denne Jesus fra Nasaret for å se om det er sannheten. For å se om dette store Guds arbeid kommer fra Gud, åpne opp Bibelen din og hold deg til den; det er slik du gjør det.

text:
So, he just took and squeezed up the old garbage can, and here he went, determined to see Jesus. Set it down by the tree; there he was, garment all full of garbage.
God knows how to do things, how to humble you, you know. Sometimes He does it in different ways, but He knows how to do it. He knows how to take that starch out of you. He knows how to make you the man that you ought to be; He knows the inside of you. You got a lot of put-on, a lot of glamour, a lot of outside; "Oh, my business is better than the other car lot over there." God knows how to take that out of you sometimes; He does it in great ways, in different ways.
text_no:
Så han tok og klemte sammen den gamle søppeldunken og dro av sted, fast bestemt på å se Jesus. Han satte den ned ved treet, med klærne fulle av søppel.
Gud vet hvordan ting skal gjøres, hvordan Han skal ydmyke deg. Noen ganger gjør Han det på ulike måter, men Han vet hvordan. Han vet hvordan Han skal ta bort stoltheten din og gjøre deg til den mannen du bør være; Han kjenner din indre natur. Du kan ha mye ytre fasade, en del glamour; "Å, min virksomhet er bedre enn bruktbilforretningen der borte." Gud vet hvordan Han skal fjerne det fra deg; Han gjør det på store og forskjellige måter.

text:
So, He had Zacchaeus standing there by the tree. So, Zacchaeus thought, "Well," and they laughed, and they was going on down to make fun of this Prophet; on down the street they went. And he said, "Now, looky here, my reputation's ruined. Here I am standing here; my garment's ruined; my reputation's ruined."
But you're just then getting in condition to find Jesus, when you lose that reputation that you think is so high, and so classy, and everything; you're just about ready then to find Jesus. When all that starch has been taken out of you, then is when you're ready to find Jesus.
So, he says, "I'll get up in the tree before someone else comes." So, he climbs, and he twists, and he gets up the tree. Finally he gets up there, and "No, that limb won't hold me." He looks where two limbs meet (come down to a "Y"), he said, "Right here's the place for me to sit, a good solid place." That's right. That's a good place for you to sit, where two ways meet: yours and God's. There's where the change comes.
text_no:
Jesus hadde Zacchaeus stående ved treet. Zacchaeus tenkte: "Vel," og de lo og gjorde narr av denne Profeten mens de gikk videre nedover gaten. Han sa: "Nå, se her, mitt rykte er ødelagt. Her står jeg; min kappe er skitten; mitt rykte er ødelagt."
Når du mister det ryktet du mener er så høyt og eksklusivt, er du nesten klar til å møte Jesus. Når all stolthet er fjernet fra deg, er du virkelig klar til å finne Jesus.
Så sier han: "Jeg skal klatre opp i treet før noen andre kommer." Han klatrer og vrir seg opp i treet. Til slutt finner han et sted og sier: "Nei, den grenen holder meg ikke." Han ser der hvor to grener møtes (danner en "Y") og sier: "Her er det et godt sted for meg å sitte, et solid sted." Det er riktig. Det er et godt sted for deg å sitte, der hvor din vei møter Guds vei. Det er der forandringen skjer.

text:
So, he sits down on the limb, sitting there, garbage stink all over him, splinters in his hands: a businessman of the city, Zacchaeus, the great man. But he's determined to see Jesus. God can get you in some horrible shape sometimes; but if you get that way and determined to see Jesus, you'll see Him.
So, sitting there picking his splinters out, he said, "You know, I just remembered, Rebekah told me that that man could stand in an audience of people and could discern the thoughts of the people. You know what? I'm going to play a trick on Him. I'm going to camouflage myself; He'll never see me in this tree and nobody else will."
So, he gets the limbs, and he pulls them all around him, makes a good camouflage all around him. And when he did, he left one little leaf here so he could raise that leaf and look out, like a little window, you see. Said, "Nobody will ever see me up here where I'm sitting now." Don't you worry; God knows right what perch you're sitting on. Exactly.
text_no:
Så sitter han der på grenen, med søppelstank over seg og fliser i hendene: en forretningsmann fra byen, Sakkeus, den store mannen. Men han er bestemt på å se Jesus. Gud kan føre deg i noen forferdelige situasjoner; men hvis du blir slik og er bestemt på å se Jesus, vil du se Ham.
Mens han plukker flisene ut av hendene, tenker han, "Jeg husker at Rebekka fortalte meg at den mannen kunne stå i en folkemengde og være i stand til å skjelne folks tanker. Vet du hva? Jeg skal spille et sprøt med Ham. Jeg skal kamuflere meg; Han vil aldri se meg i dette treet, og det vil ingen andre heller."
Så han drar grenene rundt seg og lager en god kamuflasje. Han etterlater et lite blad som han kan løfte for å kikke ut, som et lite vindu. "Ingen vil noen gang se meg her oppe hvor jeg sitter nå," tenker han. Ikke vær bekymret; Gud vet akkurat hvilken gren du sitter på. Akkurat.

text:
So, he sat there, waited awhile; the noise got closer and closer. And after while he said, "Now…" The devil sitting there talking to him, said, "Now, aren't you a pretty looking sight, one of the most outstanding businessmen of the city; you know all the doctors; you've played golf with all the different societies and so forth; and here you are sitting here, garbage all over you, sitting up in a tree to watch a holy-roller preacher pass by. What a disgrace you've come to."
Well, he kept picking the splinters. After a while he heard a noise; he looked; coming around the corner, here come a great strong-looking man walking in the front, kind of baldheaded and big shoulders back: the apostle Peter. "Stand aside, folks; we're sorry; the Master's on His way this morning, and He's been up late in the night; we're sorry, He can't speak with you this morning."
text_no:
Så, han satt der og ventet en stund; lyden kom nærmere og nærmere. Etter hvert sa han, "Nå..." Djevelen som satt der og snakket til ham, sa: "Nå, er du ikke et tragisk syn? En av byens mest fremtredende forretningsmenn, du kjenner alle legene; du har spilt golf med alle de forskjellige foreningene og så videre; og her sitter du, med søppel rundt deg, oppe i et tre for å se en hellighetsforkynner gå forbi. For en skam du har blitt."
Vel, han fortsatte å plukke på flisene. Etter en stund hørte han en lyd; han så opp; rundt hjørnet kom en stor, kraftig mann gående foran, litt skallet med brede skuldre: apostelen Peter. "Flytt dere til side, folkens; vi beklager, Mesteren er på vei denne morgenen, og Han har vært oppe sent i natt; vi beklager at Han ikke kan tale med dere dette morgenen."

text:
Here comes eleven others on the side, "Stand back, folks; we are sorry to have to do this. We don't mean to be rude, but we are sorry, the Master's coming right down. He's very tired. He's not a very big man after all; and He's a small man of stature. And He's been up much of the night. And He's been praying much for the sick; and virtue's gone from Him. So we're sorry, but you'll just have to stand aside."
And as they pushed the crowd back, Zacchaeus pulled up his leaf and he looked; he said, "Now, isn't that ridiculous." So, his eyes cast over this way, and there stood a friend of his, a man that had a little sick girl, and the doctor had told this man that that baby can only live just a few days. The best specialist in Jericho said, "That baby can only live just a few days longer. It has a tremendous fever, just the first breath of cold air would set up more infection and would kill the child."
text_no:
Her kommer elleve andre på siden, "Stå tilbake, folkens. Vi beklager å måtte gjøre dette. Vi mener ikke å være uhøflige, men beklager, Mesteren kommer rett ned. Han er veldig sliten. Han er tross alt ikke en veldig stor mann; Han er liten av vekst. Og Han har vært oppe store deler av natten. Han har bedt mye for de syke, og kraft har gått ut fra Ham. Så vi beklager, men dere må bare holde dere til side."
Mens de dyttet folkemengden tilbake, trakk Sakkeus opp et blad og kikket. Han sa, "Nå, er ikke det latterlig." Blikket hans falt over denne veien, og der stod en venn av ham, en mann som hadde en liten syk jente. Legen hadde sagt til denne mannen at barnet kun kunne leve i noen få dager til. Den beste spesialisten i Jeriko sa, "Det barnet kan bare leve noen få dager til. Hun har en enorm feber, og bare det første åndedraget med kald luft vil forårsake mer infeksjon og drepe barnet."

text:
And Zacchaeus said, "The very idea of a man would defy the intelligence of this mighty doctor, that would take that baby from the bed to come down here to visit such a ridiculous sight as this. Why, it's suicide. I'm telling you; he ought to go to the psychopathic ward. Why, it's terrible to think that a man would be so tore up, would be… He must be a psychic neurotic to bring that baby out of the bed, when a doctor told him that a little cold air would kill that child. And here he's got that lovely little girl down here and depriving her of the next two or three days of her life, that she could've stayed, out here to meet this fanatic. Oh, it's ridiculous."
And I see the little mother holding the baby, and she's patting it and kissing it, and the little baby almost unconscious. And then she says, "Dad, you hold the baby; I'll try to meet Him."
Zacchaeus looking through his little leaf watching it you know, "I'll see what happens."
text_no:
Og Sakkeus sa: "Tenk at en mann ville våge å utfordre denne store legens intelligens, ved å ta med seg den syke babyen hit for å være vitne til et slikt latterlig skue. Det er jo selvmord. Jeg sier deg; han burde sendes til psykiatrisk avdeling. Det er forferdelig å tenke at en mann kan være så ute av balanse. Han må være en psykisk nevrotiker for å ta babyen ut av sengen, når legen sa at litt kald luft ville drepe barnet. Og her har han den nydelige lille jenta med seg, frarøver henne de siste dagene av livet, alt for å møte denne fanatikeren. Det er latterlig."
Jeg ser den lille moren holde babyen, klappe og kysse den, mens den lille babyen er nesten bevisstløs. Hun sier: "Pappa, du holder babyen; jeg skal prøve å møte Ham."
Sakkeus kikker gjennom sine små blader og tenker: "Jeg skal se hva som skjer."

text:
So, after awhile here come a man around the corner; There was no beauty we should desire Him, might've been a little stoop-shouldered fellow. As He walked around the corner, it was the Prophet from Galilee. And the first view that Zacchaeus got of Him, his mind was changed. He said, "There's something about that man that's different."
This little mother rushed up, and the big strong apostle said, "Lady, I'm sorry."
She said, "But, kind sir, my little baby is at the point of death. Won't you just permit me to let the Master just touch the baby as He passes by?"
"Well, madam, I'm sorry, there's thousands of others standing here in the same way; the Master's very tired and weary. He's going up for His meal now, and I would not have you to bother Him."
text_no:
Så, etter en stund kom det en mann rundt hjørnet. Det var ingen skjønnhet vi skulle begjære i Ham; kanskje en litt lutrygget fyr. Da Han kom rundt hjørnet, var det Profeten fra Galilea. Og ved første blikk på Ham, endret Zakkeus sitt syn. Han sa: "Det er noe annerledes med den mannen."
Denne lille moren stormet opp, men den store, sterke apostelen sa: "Dame, jeg beklager."
Hun sa: "Men, kjære herre, min lille baby er døden nær. Kan Du ikke bare la Mesteren berøre babyen idet Han går forbi?"
"Vel, frue, jeg beklager, men det er tusenvis av andre her som står i samme situasjon. Mesteren er veldig sliten og trett. Han skal nå opp for å spise, og jeg vil ikke at du skal forstyrre Ham."

text:
And here He comes with His head bowed, walking, quietly, meekly. And His little ears caught that cry of that mother. He always hears it. No matter how faint it is, He knows every need that you have need of. Not one little prayer can be said without Him knowing it. She heard Him, saying, "Oh, Jehovah, be kind to your handmaid. My baby is dying. Let Your servant come near."
Zacchaeus said, "I'll see what takes place."
And she rushes on past the apostle, and she falls at the Master's feet, said, "Master, my little baby is very sick. I'm sorry to bother You; You look so weary and tired; but if You'll just lay Your hand on my baby, it'll satisfy us. We been throwed from the church now, because we brought the baby. The doctor said he would never return again if we mixed up with this at all. But oh, Master, I believe that You are the Messiah; I believe that You are the Son of the living God. That's why I brought my baby." Could He turn that down? No, sir.
Said, "Peter, hold your peace. Bring the child here."
text_no:
Og her kommer Han med hodet senket, gående stille og ydmykt. Hans små ører fanget moderens rop. Han hører alltid. Uansett hvor lavt det er, vet Han om alle behovene dine. Ikke en eneste liten bønn kan bli sagt uten at Han vet det. Hun hørte Ham og sa: "Å, Jehova, vær nådig mot Din tjenestekvinne. Mitt barn er døende. La Din tjener komme nær."
Sakkeus sa, "Jeg skal se hva som skjer."
Hun haster forbi apostelen og faller for Mesterens føtter, og sier, "Mester, mitt lille barn er veldig syk. Jeg beklager å forstyrre Deg; Du ser så trett og sliten ut; men hvis Du bare legger Din hånd på mitt barn, vil det være nok for oss. Vi har blitt kastet ut fra menigheten nå, fordi vi tok med barnet. Legen sa at han aldri ville komme tilbake hvis vi blandet oss med dette. Men å, Mester, jeg tror at Du er Messias; jeg tror at Du er Den levende Guds Sønn. Derfor tok jeg med mitt barn." Kunne Han avvise det? Nei, sir.
Han sa, "Peter, hold fred. Bring barnet hit."

text:
Here comes the father through the crowd as everyone stands watching; the baby was well known in the city. And when they brought the little baby there wrapped in the blanket, the Master merely touched the little blanket where the baby was laying; all of a sudden the little girl raised up, and let out a big joyful scream, and it throwed the blankets, and grabbed her little rope, and went skipping the rope. Zacchaeus said, "It must be real. Only God could do that."
And as they bowed politely to the Master, He went on down the street. Zacchaeus' heart was beating heavy; He thought, "Here He comes; here He comes. Now, He can never see me, but I'll see Him. So, I will raise up this leaf, and I'll watch Him. He don't know where I am. 'Course, I'm a businessman; He won't have nothing to do with me anyhow, but I wished He would. But I'm hid now. So, I'll just watch Him as He passes by."
text_no:
Her kommer faren gjennom mengden mens alle står og ser på; babyen var godt kjent i byen. Da de brakte den lille babyen, innpakket i et teppe, rørte Mesteren bare ved teppet der babyen lå. Plutselig reiste den lille jenta seg opp, utstøtte et stort, gledelig skrik, kastet teppet av seg, grep sitt lille tau og begynte å hoppe tau. Sakkeus sa: "Det må være ekte. Bare Gud kunne gjøre det."
Mens de bøyde seg høflig for Mesteren, fortsatte Han nedover gaten. Sakkeus' hjerte slo tungt; Han tenkte: "Her kommer Han, her kommer Han. Nå kan Han aldri se meg, men jeg vil se Ham. Så jeg løfter opp dette bladet og ser på Ham. Han vet ikke hvor jeg er. Selvfølgelig, jeg er en forretningsmann; Han vil ikke ha noe med meg å gjøre uansett, men jeg skulle ønske Han ville. Men nå er jeg skjult. Så jeg vil bare se på Ham mens Han går forbi."

text:
So, he raised his leaf and begin to look. Jesus walking along with His head bowed, He stopped all at once, looked up in the tree, said, "Zacchaeus, come down out of that tree. I'm going home with you today."
He didn't only know he was there; He knew his name. He knew his heart. He's the same yesterday, today, and forever. Zacchaeus was convinced; he knew who He was; he knew that was the Messiah. Zacchaeus dropped out of the tree; he said, "Lord, if I've robbed any man, I'll make it good. I'll do anything; I'll just do anything."
Rebekah knew all of this; she's already had dinner ready for Him to come when He's going up to his house; salvation had come to his house.
text_no:
Han løftet bladet og begynte å se. Jesus gikk langs med hodet bøyd, stoppet plutselig, så opp i treet og sa: "Sakkeus, kom ned fra det treet. Jeg skal hjem til deg i dag."
Han visste ikke bare at Sakkeus var der; Han kjente også navnet hans. Han kjente hjertet hans. Jesus er den samme i går, i dag og for alltid. Sakkeus ble overbevist; han visste hvem Han var; han visste at dette var Messias. Sakkeus klatret ned fra treet og sa: "Herre, hvis jeg har bedratt noen, skal jeg gjøre opp for det. Jeg skal gjøre hva som helst; jeg skal virkelig gjøre hva som helst."
Rebekka visste alt dette; hun hadde allerede middagen klar for Ham da Han dro til hans hus. Frelsen var kommet til hans hjem.

text:
Maybe there's a Christian businessman or a man sitting here this morning that Rebekah has prayed for. You knew He was to pass this way this morning. He knows where you're at; He sees you. No matter how much leaf you're raising up; He knows who you are; He knows right where you're sitting; He knows all about it. I wonder while we bow our heads just a moment in prayer…
Father God, we heard that You were to pass this way this morning. I don't know these people; there may be a Zacchaeus sitting here. I do not know. Maybe a dear mother has prayed for her wandering boy. Oh, he's been blessed, truly. He's in business; he's got a family now. Mother's done gone on, but her prayers are still real. We heard such a testimony awhile ago of a wandering boy, dear old daddy who preached the Gospel. And oh, You're always near.
text_no:
Kanskje det er en kristen forretningsmann eller en mann som sitter her i dag, som Rebekka har bedt for. Du visste at Han skulle komme forbi her i dag. Han vet hvor du er; Han ser deg. Uansett hvor mye løv du løfter opp, vet Han hvem du er; Han vet nøyaktig hvor du sitter; Han vet alt om det. Jeg undrer meg, mens vi bøyer våre hoder i bønn et øyeblikk...
Far Gud, vi hørte at Du skulle komme denne veien i dag. Jeg kjenner ikke disse menneskene; kanskje det er en Sakkeus som sitter her. Jeg vet ikke. Kanskje en kjær mor har bedt for sin bortkomne sønn. Å, han har virkelig blitt velsignet. Han driver forretninger; han har en familie nå. Mor har gått bort, men hennes bønner er fortsatt virkelige. Vi hørte et slikt vitnesbyrd for en stund siden om en forvillet gutt, en kjære gammel pappa som prekte Evangeliet. Og åh, Du er alltid nær.

text:
Maybe that boy or girl, whatever it might've been, wandered far away, but they just drifted in the door this morning, said, "Well, I heard that He was to pass this way." Just take that leaf away from them, Lord, this morning. Call them by name and say, "Child of Mine, I've come in answer to your mother, or your wife, or father's prayer. I've come to receive you. I'm going back home with you this morning. I'm going to leave this New Yorker restaurant. I'm going home with you. Not only am I just going for dinner; I'm going to abide with you, till I bring you to mother and dad again."
O Christ, who knows the heart of man, who knew that Zacchaeus was in the tree; You knowed his name; You knowed his thoughts; You're speaking to our conscience this morning and to our hearts. We pray, God, that You'll unfold our sins to us. And may that Zacchaeus slip lovely down from the tree just now, off of the high perch, come down, and humble himself at the foot of the cross to say, "Jesus, I come."
text_no:
Kanskje den gutten eller jenta, hvem det enn måtte ha vært, hadde vandret langt bort, men de kom tilfeldigvis inn døren denne morgenen og sa: "Vel, jeg hørte at Han skulle komme forbi her." Ta bort den barrieren fra dem, Herre, denne morgenen. Kall dem ved navn og si: "Mitt barn, Jeg har kommet som svar på morens, eller konens, eller farens bønn. Jeg har kommet for å motta deg. Jeg skal gå hjem med deg denne morgenen. Jeg skal forlate denne New Yorker-restauranten. Jeg skal hjem med deg. Ikke bare for å spise middag; Jeg skal bli hos deg til Jeg bringer deg tilbake til mor og far igjen."
O Kristus, som kjenner menneskenes hjerter, som visste at Sakkeus var i treet; Du kjente hans navn; Du kjente hans tanker; Du taler til vår samvittighet og våre hjerter denne morgenen. Vi ber, Gud, at Du avdekker våre synder for oss. Måtte den Sakkeus ydmykt komme ned fra treet nå, ned fra sin høye posisjon, og bøye seg ved korsets fot og si: "Jesus, jeg kommer."

text:
While we have our heads bowed, would there be one here this morning? You might think this is not appropriate at a breakfast. But a Zacchaeus, are you here? Mrs. Zacchaeus, are you here? Would you want Him to go home with you this morning from this breakfast to abide with you forever, carry you over Jordan someday safely on His lovely wings. Would you just raise your hand to Him and say, "By this I mean to come to Christ." Would you put up your hand just now? Every head bowed, just let the Holy Spirit and myself see these hands.
Come on, Zacchaeus, put… God bless you. All right. God bless you, God bless you. That's right, Zacchaeus. Come on, raise up the leaf; He's here. That's Him talking to you. God bless you. God bless you, brother. God bless you. Come on, Zacchaeus; He knows right where you are; you're right there.
text_no:
Mens vi har hodene bøyd, er det noen her i dag? Du tenker kanskje at dette ikke er passende ved en frokost. Men en Sakkeus, er du her? Fru Sakkeus, er du her? Ønsker du at Han skal gå hjem med deg fra denne frokosten for å bli hos deg for alltid, og bære deg trygt over Jordan en dag på Hans vakre vinger? Ville du bare løfte din hånd til Ham og si: "Med dette mener jeg å komme til Kristus." Vil du løfte hånden din nå? Hvert hode bøyd, la bare Den Hellige Ånd og jeg se disse hendene.
Kom igjen, Sakkeus, løft... Gud velsigne deg. Greit. Gud velsigne deg, Gud velsigne deg. Det er riktig, Sakkeus. Kom igjen, løft løvet; Han er her. Det er Ham som snakker til deg. Gud velsigne deg. Gud velsigne deg, bror. Gud velsigne deg. Kom igjen, Sakkeus; Han vet nøyaktig hvor du er; du er rett der.

text:
Oh, I know you've prospered; your business has been great. But why don't you take Jesus with you this morning? Won't you let Him go with you? He's come for you. He's revealing to you now who you are, making you know that little crooked deal that you did the other day, that little bad word that you spoke, that little smart-aleck thing you said to that woman or that man, He knows it. He's speaking to you; that's Him talking to your conscience. Aren't you ashamed of yourself?
"Lord, from this day, from this breakfast on, I make a covenant with You, that someday when life is over, and I sit at that great table yonder, the King of kings comes out and wipes all tears from the eyes, I want Him to say, 'It was well done, my good and faithful servant; enter into the joys of the Lord.'"
text_no:
Å, jeg vet at du har hatt suksess; virksomheten din har gått strålende. Men hvorfor tar du ikke Jesus med deg i dag? Vil du ikke la Ham bli med deg? Han har kommet for deg. Han åpenbarer nå hvem du er, og lar deg forstå den lille uærligheten du gjorde forleden dag, det stygge ordet du sa, eller den frekke bemerkningen til den kvinnen eller mannen. Han kjenner til det. Han taler til deg; det er Ham som henvender seg til din samvittighet. Er du ikke skamfull?
"Herre, fra denne dag, fra denne frokosten og fremover, inngår jeg en pakt med Deg. En dag, når livet er over og jeg sitter ved det store bordet der borte, når Kongenes Konge kommer ut og tørker bort alle tårer fra øynene, ønsker jeg at Han skal si: 'Det var godt gjort, min gode og tro tjener; gå inn til din Herres glede.'"

text:
Will you raise your hand, one that hasn't? Say, "Remember me, God; I now raise my hand to Thee. I know You're revealing to me my wrongs and my sins now. You know my heart. I want You to go home with me from this meeting this morning and live with me, go in my business office. I want to go back and tell all my employees that I'm a changed man. I met Someone down to the breakfast."
"What breakfast?"
"Oh, down where the Christian Businessmen was having a meeting. I was sitting at a table, and all of a sudden Something spoke to my heart; my sins stood out like mountains before me. In a low voice I whispered, 'God, forgive me.' It all cleared away, and peace come to my troubled soul. I believe I'll rest tonight. I have peace like the river. I want you employees to know that I'm going to be a different man now."
text_no:
Vil du rekke opp hånden, en som ikke har gjort det ennå? Si, "Husk meg, Gud; Jeg hever nå hånden til Deg. Jeg vet at Du nå åpenbarer mine feil og synder for meg. Du kjenner mitt hjerte. Jeg vil at Du skal bli med meg hjem fra dette møtet i dag morges og bo sammen med meg, være med i mitt kontor. Jeg vil gå tilbake og fortelle alle mine ansatte at jeg er en forandret mann. Jeg møtte Noen til frokosten."
"Hvilken frokost?"
"Å, der nede hvor de kristne forretningsmennene hadde et møte. Jeg satt ved et bord, og plutselig talte Noe til mitt hjerte; mine synder stod frem som fjell foran meg. Lavt hvisket jeg, 'Gud, tilgi meg.' Det hele klarte opp, og fred kom til min urolige sjel. Jeg tror jeg vil hvile i natt. Jeg har fred som en elv. Jeg vil at dere ansatte skal vite at jeg nå vil være en annerledes mann."

text:
Go home and see wife and say, "Wife, your prayer has been answered; I met Jesus this morning. I heard He's going to pass that way; I never did think it come that way. But I was just sitting there, and all at once something struck me. It could only be Him. I've been hard-hearted, I've been indifferent. Oh, I've been a church member, sure, but something happened. He come home with me, wife. I'm going to treat you different from now on. You watch and see if I don't. I'm going to be different to the children too; I'll never drink no more. I don't know why, but I've tried to turn new pages, but something's happened to me. I'm going to be different now."
"Oh, I've professed to be a Christian; I've carried on, and used bad language, and went out into worldly things, and to movies, and bioscopes, and I've watched vulgar programs. I've cracked dirty jokes, taken social drinks with my competitors and things; but I'll never do it again. Something happened; Jesus come home with me."
text_no:
